title: Canonical scratch home .bee/tmp/ + `bee tmp sweep` verb (D1, D2)
action: Per CONTEXT D1/D2 (docs/history/tree-hygiene/CONTEXT.md). Build the home and its broom. (A) New CLI verb `bee tmp sweep` in skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs + a new lib module skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/scratch.mjs (mirror both to .bee/bin), registered in lib/command-registry.mjs with a runnable examples[0]. Flags: --feature <slug> (sweep that feature's scratch), --before <ISO> (age cutoff), --all, --dry-run, --json. SAFETY (highest risk in the plan): the sweep is ROOT-RESTRICTED — it may only ever delete inside <repo>/.bee/tmp/ and <repo>/.bee/spikes/; every candidate path is canonically resolved and re-checked to be contained in one of those two roots before removal (reuse the containment idiom the write-guard already uses; symlink escapes must be refused, not followed). With NO flags it REFUSES (typed) — no default purge, same discipline as `decisions archive`. Default target set when --all is absent: scratch belonging to features that are closed (state lanes phase compounding-complete) or that no longer exist as lanes, plus anything older than --before; a LIVE feature's scratch is only swept when named explicitly via --feature. Output/JSON reports files removed, bytes freed, and (in --dry-run) exactly what WOULD go. (B) Wire the sweep into the close path: bee-compounding's close step calls it for the finishing feature (prose + the actual call site if compounding has one), and the session-finish checklist in AGENTS.block.md/doctrine mentions it — coordinate with th-6 which owns the doctrine rule, so here just make the verb exist and be callable. (C) Add `.bee/tmp/` to the managed gitignore block ONLY if th-5 has not landed it yet — check first, do not duplicate the entry. RED-FIRST: write the checks first in a new skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_scratch.mjs (standalone convention, temp fixture roots — never the real repo), run red, record `cells verify --id th-4 --passed false`, then implement. Checks: no-flag refusal; --dry-run removes nothing and lists accurately; closed-feature scratch swept while live-feature scratch survives; --feature on a live feature sweeps it; refusal when a computed path escapes the two roots (incl. a symlink pointing outside); docs/** and .bee/cells/ and .bee/decisions.jsonl are NEVER touched under any flag combination; byte/file counts in the JSON match reality. Mirror, render, release_manifest --write/--check. TARGETED verify only (test_scratch.mjs + test_bee_cli.mjs for the registry example) — no full chain (verify ladder; orchestrator runs it at wave close).

truths: sweep refuses with no flags (no default purge)
sweep can only ever delete inside .bee/tmp/ or .bee/spikes/ — an escaping or symlinked path is refused, not followed
--dry-run deletes nothing and its listing matches what a real run would remove
closed/absent-feature scratch is swept; a live feature's scratch survives unless named with --feature
docs/**, .bee/cells/, .bee/decisions.jsonl untouched under every flag combination
RED-FIRST failing run recorded before implementation
prohibitions: Never delete outside the two allowed roots — no exceptions, no flags that widen it
Never follow a symlink out of the roots
No timer/daemon/auto-sweep — deletion rides an explicit call

trace.outcome: Canonical scratch home .bee/tmp/ + root-restricted  (symlinked roots refused, not followed); compounding close sweeps the finished feature
trace.verify_output:
PASS  scratchRoots REFUSES a symlinked .bee/spikes ROOT — never relocates the authority root to the symlink target
PASS  REPRO (judge): .bee/spikes -> repo root, `tmp sweep --all` must NOT delete .bee/cells/ or .bee/decisions.jsonl
PASS  REPRO (judge): .bee/tmp -> docs/, `tmp sweep --all` must NOT delete docs/history
PASS  dry-run/real-run parity: a symlinked .bee/tmp root is refused THE SAME WAY under --dry-run as under a real run
PASS  plan-then-swap: a plan computed while .bee/tmp is real does not license a later run after the root is swapped to a symlink
PASS  finding 4 (pinned): a CLOSED record sweeps even when --before predates its mtime — --before never age-gates closed scratch

25 passed, 0 failed
---
PASS  malformed advisor_ref reads as missing — the gate verb refuses cleanly, never crashes

229 passed, 0 failed

checks.evidence: bee.mjs:3184-3189 throws before runSweep at :3190; tests assert non-zero exit and dir still on disk.

REAL ESCAPE reproduced via the shipped CLI in a mkdtemp fixture: with .bee/spikes a symlink to the repo root, `tmp sweep --all` removed name='.bee' (cells/, decisions.jsonl, state.json, onboarding.json). Variant: .bee/tmp -> docs/ removed docs/history. ROOT CAUSE scratch.mjs:55-61 scratchRoots() realpaths the ROOT symlinks themselves and stores the target as `real`; containedRoot() :68-72 proves containment against the moved root; listEntries() :142-152 readdirs rootInfo.abs. The literal <repo>/.bee/{tmp,spikes} prefix is never enforced.

runSweep skips the pre-removal containment re-check under dryRun (scratch.mjs:253-261 `if (!dryRun)`), so dry-run listed both '.bee' and 'docs' while the real run refused 'docs'. Divergence is in the safe direction but advertises deliverables as sweepable.
scratch.mjs:190-220 live=>skip, terminal-record=>qualify, no-record=>needs --before. CAVEAT: --before does not age-gate closed-record scratch (documented :21-23 but untested and surprising).
Same root-symlink reproduction deleted .bee/cells/x-1.json and .bee/decisions.jsonl; the .bee/tmp->docs variant deleted docs/history. Existing guard test only exercises non-symlinked roots.
trace.attempts[0] fail f532b7d59d5d 14:44:32Z before pass 14:51:58Z; test file mtime precedes lib creation by ~6min.
Violated by the root-symlink case; deletion physically hit <repo>/.bee/ and <repo>/docs/.
Entry-level symlinks correctly refused (:175-182) but the ROOT symlink is followed (:58-60, :145).
runSweep called exactly once from handleTmpSweep; no scheduler anywhere.
--feature never path-joined, only compared to a dirent name; probes over ../../../etc, .., /etc, '', *, ** all removed nothing.
containedRoot re-run per candidate immediately before rmSync (:253-261), refusals collected. Residual parent-swap raciness noted.
Roots from module constant SCRATCH_ROOT_RELS; no flag threaded into root selection.

Mutant with the pre-removal re-check DELETED still passes 19/19 — the most safety-critical line is unpinned. Missing: symlinked-root case, closed-record newer than --before, dry-run/real-run listing divergence.
test_lib_mirror 26 files byte-identical; registry example runnable. NOTE the mirrored .bee/bin copy carries the same escape — the live CLI is affected.
skipped[] with live|absent_no_before|absent_not_old_enough is honest and conservative; absent scratch never swept without --before.
grep of skills/bee-compounding/ for sweep/.bee/tmp returns nothing; the close-path wiring required by action (B) is absent.
.bee/tmp/ correctly left for th-5 which owns GITIGNORE_BLOCK_PATTERNS; no duplicate added.

Orchestrator re-ran the iteration-1 repro through the SHIPPED .bee/bin/bee.mjs in a fresh mktemp fixture (.bee/spikes -> fixture root, real .bee/cells/x-1.json + .bee/decisions.jsonl + docs/history/CONTEXT.md present): output removed=[], bytes_freed=0, refused_roots=[{rel:'.bee/spikes', reason:'symlinked_root'}]; all three deliverables verified present afterwards by ls. The escape that deleted them at iteration 1 is closed.

Targeted mutation removing ONLY the `return { rel, abs, refused: 'symlinked_root' }` line (scratch.mjs:88) still refuses the same attack, with reason 'not_a_directory' — the lstat-based literal-root check is an independent second layer, so the safety property does not rest on one line.

Mutation neutering every isSymbolicLink() call in scratch.mjs makes the suite FAIL (24 passed, 1 failed — 'computeSweepPlan --all refuses the symlink escape (refused_escapes)… Error: escape must be reported in refusedEscapes, got []'). The iteration-1 finding (a mutant passing 19/19) no longer reproduces: broken symlink handling is now caught.

The suite pins the PROPERTY (escape refused, nothing removed, deliverables intact) rather than the specific reason string — which is why the single-line mutation stays green while still being safe. Judged correct: over-pinning a label would make the tests brittle without adding safety.

node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_scratch.mjs -> 25 passed, 0 failed (orchestrator re-run, up from 19 at iteration 1); no-flag refusal re-verified through the CLI with a typed error naming the four flags and zero mutation.

HONEST SCOPE: the iteration-2 independent judge dispatch was interrupted by the user mid-run; these checks were performed by the orchestrator (different model from the sonnet builder) with concrete reproductions and two code mutations, not by reading the builder's report. Items NOT independently re-verified this round: compounding close-path wiring (action B), dry-run/real-run listing parity, and the --before semantics decision — all reported by the builder, none of them safety-critical, and all re-checkable at wave close.
